expectations.text: Flags the shared VLAN as non-compliance with Control 1.1 (SWIFT Environment Protection — dedicated secure zone segregated from general IT)
expectations.evidence: "1.1 | SWIFT Environment Protection | 🔴 Not Implemented | ... | SWIFT zone exists but sits on a shared VLAN with other internal systems — this is dual-homing/lack of isolation, a direct breach of the core requirement that the Secure Zone be isolated by deny-by-default firewalls."

expectations.text: Flags software OTP as a gap against Control 4.2 (MFA) and flags the logging state against Control 6.4 (no log review; 6-month retention is insufficient — SWIFT guidance expects at least 12 months)
expectations.evidence: "4.2 | Multi-Factor Authentication | 🔴 Not Implemented | ... Software-based OTP (authenticator app) is in use. Per CSCF v2026, software OTP does not satisfy this control..." and "6.4 | Log and Monitoring | 🔴 Not Implemented | ... (1) retention is 6 months, vs. the required minimum of 1 year online / 3 years total; (2) logs are collected but not reviewed..."

expectations.text: Flags the absence of a SWIFT-specific incident response plan as a gap against Control 7.1 (Cyber Incident Response Planning)
expectations.evidence: "7.1 | Cyber Incident Response Planning | 🔴 Not Implemented | ... A general IT incident response plan exists, but there is no SWIFT-specific IRP. The control requires explicit coverage of SWIFT notification obligations..."

expectations.text: Correctly recognizes named individual operator accounts as compliant with Control 5.1 (Logical Access Control) rather than flagging them as a gap
expectations.evidence: "Named individual operator accounts (no shared accounts) is a genuine strength and satisfies the core anti-repudiation requirement." — the specific fact given is credited as satisfying the requirement, not flagged as a gap (the overall control is rated Partial only due to other unprovided sub-elements like access reviews, not due to the named-accounts practice itself).

expectations.text: Delivers a prioritized remediation plan (severity/priority ordering with concrete actions and rough sequencing), consistent with the requested gap-assessment format
expectations.evidence: "Part 3 — Prioritised Remediation Plan" with "Priority 1 — Critical (target: 30 days)", "Priority 2 — High (target: 60–90 days)", "Priority 3 — Medium (target: 90–120 days...)", "Priority 4 — Attestation Readiness", each with numbered actions, owners, and rationale.
